heroHeadline: Liberation Day Anniversary: One Year of Tariff War; Iran War Day 33 — Trump Claims Navy 'Gone,' Oil Pulls Back to $104; SCOTUS Hears Birthright Citizenship Case; Trump Approval Below 40%
heroSubtitle: April 2 marks one year since Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariff executive order — SCOTUS struck down the IEEPA tariffs in February 2026, the US trade deficit hit an all-time high of $1.2T, and 89,000 manufacturing jobs were lost in the first 10 months. On Day 33 of the Iran war, oil pulled back to ~$104/barrel as markets assessed Trump's April 1 primetime address declaring Iran's navy 'gone' and air force 'in ruins' — Iran's foreign ministry called the speech 'psychological warfare' and denied making any ceasefire request. The Supreme Court heard oral arguments on Trump's birthright citizenship EO with bipartisan skepticism from the bench; Trump became the first sitting president to attend SCOTUS oral arguments in person. Trump's approval rating fell below 40% for the first time in his second term.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, SCOTUSblog, NPR, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute.
